title: Some Useful Signal Operations
summary: This page presents Example 1.5 demonstrating time reversal of a signal x(t) = e^(t/2), showing how x(-t) = e^(-t/2) is obtained by reflecting the signal about the vertical axis. It then introduces combined signal operations, explaining how the general form x(at - b) can be achieved through two different sequences of time-shifting and time-scaling operations. A concrete example using x(2t - 6) illustrates both approaches.

figures.caption: Illustration of time-reversal of a signal: (a) original signal x(t) consisting of a growing exponential e^(t/2) over the interval [-5, -1], and (b) its time-reversed version x(-t) showing a decaying exponential e^(-t/2) over the interval [1, 5].
